etymology.description: semantic 木(木) (mù, 'tree') + phonetic 卜(卜) (bǔ) -> representing simplicity and plainness associated with untreated wood
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 木(木) (mù, 'tree') signifies untreated or natural wood, conveying the notion of something simple and unembellished. The phonetic component 卜(卜) (bǔ) lends its sound to form the character. Together, these elements suggest the quality of something that remains plain or unworked, akin to raw wood.
etymology.simplification: As part of the Chinese Character Simplification Scheme, the character 樸 was simplified to 朴 by removing the additional components and retaining the simpler structure.

examples.chinese: 她的穿着风格非常朴素(她的穿著風格非常樸素)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de chuānzhuó fēnggé fēicháng pǔsù.
examples.english: Her style of dressing is very plain.

examples.chinese: 这间房子的设计很朴实(這間房子的設計很樸實)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiān fángzi de shèjì hěn pǔshí.
examples.english: The design of this house is very simple.

examples.chinese: 他过着朴素的生活(他過著樸素的生活)，不喜欢奢华(不喜歡奢華)。
examples.pinyin: Tā guòzhe pǔsù de shēnghuó, bù xǐhuān shēhuá.
examples.english: He leads a plain life and does not like luxury.
